commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `applyDoctorConfigMutation` to set `pendingChanges` false in repair mode and updates doctor repair sequencing tests to expect repaired mutations to have no pending changes.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the patched false value flows into `finalizeDoctorConfigFlow`, which writes repaired config only when `shouldRepair && pendingChanges`; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to repair the writeback regression and provide their own real behavior proof; automation cannot satisfy the external contributor proof gate for this PR.

target.title: fix(harness): avoid default Codex pre-tool loop relay
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes an unnecessary Codex Harness native PreToolUse relay in the default configuration.

`tools.loopDetection.enabled` is documented and typed as default `false`, but `nativePreToolUseMayRunLoopDetection()` treated an unset loop-detection config as enabled via `enabled !== false`. For Codex Harness, that made the runtime install a native PreToolUse hook whenever a session key existed, even when the user had not enabled tool-loop detection and no before-tool policy needed to run.

Because Codex executes native hooks as `openclaw hooks relay` subprocesses, the default path added OpenClaw-side CPU overhead on tool-heavy Codex Harness runs. Codex ACP does not use this same Codex native-hook relay path, which explains part of the Harness-vs-ACP CPU delta.

## Fix

- Treat native loop-detection PreToolUse relay work as present only when `tools.loopDetection.enabled === true`.
- Preserve PreToolUse relay behavior for explicit `before_tool_call` policy/trusted policy work.
- Preserve PreToolUse relay behavior when loop detection is explicitly enabled.
- Keep explicit `enabled: false` behavior unchanged.

## Evidence

OpenClaw 2026.5.28 CPU probe, same Codex Harness leaf, same prompt/model/runtime, 60s window, corrected sampler excluding sampler/ps overhead:

| Run | tick CPU-s | pcpu integral CPU-s | OpenClaw CLI CPU-s | OpenClaw hooks CPU-s | OpenClaw agent CPU-s |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Harness default | 9.25 | 26.320 | 3.16 | 0.44 | 3.96 |
| Harness with `tools.loopDetection.enabled=false` | 6.82 | 23.488 | 1.07 | 0.00 | 3.88 |

Interpretation:

- Disabling loop detection removed visible `openclaw-hooks` relay samples and reduced `openclaw-cli` CPU by about 66% in this 60s probe.
- The persistent `openclaw-agent` CPU did not materially change, so this patch targets the avoidable native-hook relay subprocess work, not the Harness agent wait-loop baseline.
- A copied-leaf patched runtime probe was not used as proof because copied `plugins/installs.json` retained absolute plugin install paths from the source leaf; the source-level tests below are the authoritative validation for this patch.

## Validation

Clean PR head: `28cd1e718c348e476f234e003b29e22364fa6e82`.

- PR surface after cleanup: 1 commit, 2 files only (`src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`, `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`).
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 152 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts` -> 3 files passed, 216 tests passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed.
- GitHub reports the PR as mergeable.
target.author: marchpure
